Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

> Test-Parameter: trivial envdefinitions=ONLY=810
 > Test-Parameter: testlist=sanity,sanity,sanity,sanity,sanity,sanity

FYI, splitting the parameters across multiple lines probably isn't what you want.  This will run two sessions, one with sanity running only test_810() (because it is the only test script run for a trivial session), the other with six sanity runs with all subtests.

@Andreas, if you don't mind, I can rebase the patch on top of https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/32349/ and I think some test failures will be fixed. The test failure caused by T10PI will not since the page sizes are different between client and server.

> @Andreas, if you don't mind, I can rebase the patch on top of
 > https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/32349/ and I think some test
 > failures will be fixed.

Feel free.

> The test failure caused by T10PI will not since the page sizes
> are different between client and server.

Hmm, that shouldn't be the case for the network protocol, otherwise it should be considered a bug.

Patch Set 4:

> Hmm, that shouldn't be the case for the network protocol, otherwise it should be considered a bug.

Agreed, now is the time to fix the hidden bug(s) of non-4K page sizes.

Found checksum error for OBD_CKSUM_CRC32(0x1), OBD_CKSUM_CRC32C(0x4) and OBD_CKSUM_T10IP4K(0x20). However, only the last one failed and quit. I/Os with the first two checksum type might succeeded with retry?

00000008:02020000:0.0:1564057737.824067:2800:11852:0:(osc_request.c:1720:check_write_checksum()) 132-0: lustre-OST0000-osc-c00000007498f000: BAD WRITE CHECKSUM: changed in transit before arrival at OST: from 10.9.5.62@tcp inode [0x200000401:0x3:0x0] object 0x0:2 extent [0-10239], original client csum a3b42117 (type 4), server csum 1721b4a3 (type 4), client csum now a3b42117


00000008:02020000:1.0:1564057769.860259:2800:11851:0:(osc_request.c:1720:check_write_checksum()) 132-0: lustre-OST0000-osc-c00000007498f000: BAD WRITE CHECKSUM: changed in transit before arrival at OST: from 10.9.5.62@tcp inode [0x200000401:0x3:0x0] object 0x0:2 extent [10240-20479], original client csum a9b0288 (type 20), server csum 9910288 (type 20), client csum now a9b0288

parents.subject: LU-12579 tests: allow some margin in runtests

commit.subject: LU-11729 obdclass: align to T10 sector size when generating guard
commit.message:
LU-11729 obdclass: align to T10 sector size when generating guard

Otherwise the client and server would come up with
different checksum when the page size is different.

Improve test_810 to verify all available checksum types.

Test-Parameters: trivial envdefinitions=ONLY=810 testlist=sanity,sanity,sanity
Test-Parameters: clientarch=aarch64 envdefinitions=ONLY=810 testlist=sanity,sanity
Test-Parameters: clientarch=ppc64 envdefinitions=ONLY=810 testlist=sanity,sanity
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Signed-off-by: Li Xi <lixi@ddn.com>
Signed-off-by: Li Dongyang <dongyangli@ddn.com>
Change-Id: I24117aebb277d4ddcb7787b715587e33023ebbe5
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/34043
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.super@gmail.com>
